context: Tests whether the agent uses per-container resource commands (kubectl top --containers), understands multi-container diagnostic approach, and checks resource constraints systematically.

checklist.name: Container-level metrics
checklist.description: Uses kubectl top pod with --containers flag to show per-container resource usage

checklist.name: Multi-container context
checklist.description: Documentation acknowledges pod has multiple containers and each needs separate analysis

checklist.name: Resource limits checked
checklist.description: Script or report checks resource requests and limits for each container in the manifest

checklist.name: OOM investigation
checklist.description: Checks for OOMKilled status or memory-related termination in pod status/events

checklist.name: Container-specific logs
checklist.description: Uses kubectl logs -c flag to target specific containers by name

checklist.name: Namespace specified
checklist.description: All kubectl commands include -n production namespace flag

checklist.name: Systematic approach
checklist.description: Report documents checking: overall pod resources → per-container breakdown → identify culprit

checklist.name: Resource quota context
checklist.description: Mentions or checks kubectl describe resourcequota to understand namespace-level constraints

checklist.name: Specific container ID
checklist.description: Report identifies which specific container (by name) is causing the resource spike

checklist.name: Timestamps included
checklist.description: Uses --timestamps or similar flags to show when resource issues began
